text_template: Pain is different for everyone. But where pain starts, Journavx Suzetrogene begins. Journavx is a non-opioid 50 milligram prescription tablet for adults with moderate to severe acute pain that reduces pain signals before they reach the brain. Before those signals tell the brain to feel like I punched an angry beehive and pain reduction can finally begin. Do not take Journavx with CYP3A inhibitors. Avoid use with grapefruit or liver problems as these may increase side effects. Tell your doctor about all your medicines. If you are pregnant or breastfeeding, you can't be a doctor. Speeding. Journavx may make certain medicines and birth control less effective. Side effects include itching, muscle spasms, rash, and elevated muscle enzymes. To learn more about Journavx, ask your doctor, call 1-833-589-PAIN or visit journavx.com.
